flow_points: Scotland scored 42 runs for 3 wickets in the Powerplay, making a slow start.
This early loss of 3 wickets in the Powerplay prevented Scotland from building a stable foundation.
AU Rashid delivered a crucial spell for England, taking 3 wickets for 36 runs.
Rashid's bowling sustained significant defensive pressure, proving a key turning point.
J Overton also contributed with an economical spell for England, taking 1 wicket for 24 runs.
In their chase, England made a slightly better start, scoring 43 runs for 2 wickets in their Powerplay.
T Banton anchored England's innings, scoring 63 runs from 42 balls.
Banton's innings was pivotal, contributing 40.6% of England's total runs and anchoring their victory.
England ultimately secured the win by 5 wickets.
